item.title: The boutique bank that eschews blockbuster bragging rights
item.description: Lincoln International may not be a household name but it occupies a niche in the market

item.title: To infinity and beyond, with the SpaceX IPO
item.description: Ordinary investors can at last bet directly on AI’s potential — with caveats

item.title: Jonathan Andic, the Mango heir turned homicide suspect
item.description: The son of the fashion brand’s founder is now being investigated for foul play in connection with his father’s death

item.title: Apollo makes £1.5bn bid for London-listed Bodycote
item.description: FTSE 250 components group says takeover proposal is latest in several efforts by US firm to buy its business

item.title: France seeks to join UK-German long-range missile plan
item.description: Paris warms to joint project aimed at closing conventional capability gap with Russia

item.title: How the British army simulated World War 3 in Charing Cross Tube station
item.description: Elsewhere commuters boarded trains, unaware of the exercise unfolding nearby

item.title: A gargantuan energy deal for the AI age
item.description: The combination of NextEra and Dominion is a pragmatic response to growing energy needs

item.title: Guardiola to leave Manchester City after trophy-laden decade
item.description: Charges of breaching financial rules loom over club as Catalan ends one of most successful managerial reigns in English football

item.title: CVC consortium launches €10.9bn offer for Italian drugmaker Recordati
item.description: Deal would tighten buyout group’s control of 100-year-old company and extend its bet on rare diseases

item.title: Richemont shines as demand booms for high-end jewellery
item.description: Cartier owner beats expectations as strong jewellery demand offsets weaker sales in Middle East and Africa

item.title: Starbucks grapples with backlash in South Korea after ‘Tank Day’ marketing mis-step
item.description: Promotion offered discounts on May 18, the anniversary of the 1980 Gwangju massacre

item.title: UK borrowed a higher than expected £24.3bn in April
item.description: British retail sales fall as Middle East conflict hits household spending

item.title: Legal disputes rip through oil shipping after Hormuz closure
item.description: TotalEnergies weighs lawsuit against Baltic Exchange, following Mercuria, as shipping derivative losses mount

item.title: A bitcoin miner spent $860k armouring vehicles for its bosses, a fact for which we have very little context
item.description: The best defence may in fact be defence

item.title: FirstFT: SpaceX, OpenAI and Anthropic IPOs to ignite trading frenzy
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: private equity-linked loans and Estée Lauder-Puig talks halted

item.title: The Musk banker who took a back seat to Goldman Sachs
item.description: Michael Grimes of Morgan Stanley had been widely expected to run SpaceX’s flotation

item.title: JPMorgan looks to offload exposure to $4bn in private equity-linked loans
item.description: Largest US bank in discussions over risk transfer as PE firms grapple with prolonged slowdown

item.title: As athletes gather for ‘steroid Olympics’ the real stars are the drugs
item.description: Backers of Enhanced Games hope to use event to market performance-enhancing drugs to a wider audience

item.title: Investing in the era of scarcity
item.description: Markets have yet to register the new fashion among governments for hoarding and huddling

item.title: Next Harvard endowment chief faces a private equity hangover
item.description: As CEO NP Narvekar nears retirement Ivy League fund will have to reckon with expansion into sluggish private holdings

item.title: Brookfield among PE suitors circling Gulf fertility firm
item.description: Deal could value Fakih IVF at $1bn as healthcare proves resilient Middle East sector during Iran war

item.title: The fragile maths of Uncle Sam’s energy self-sufficiency
item.description: US may produce more oil than it consumes but banning exports would be extremely complicated

item.title: Generics drugmaker Sandoz calls for EU to probe alleged China dumping
item.description: Novartis spin-off says Chinese overcapacity threatens European self-reliance and access to key antibiotic

item.title: The European cars made in China
item.description: Western automakers are taking advantage of Chinese overcapacity to export lower-cost vehicles to their home markets

item.title: BMW, SpaceX and the value of a vote
item.description: Investors welcome German car maker’s move to convert preference shares into equity with voting rights

item.title: Trump reboots foreign aid with new ‘America First’ strategy
item.description: After gutting USAID, Washington is striking transactional money-for-data deals that some critics liken to ‘recolonisation’

item.title: The commodities trader aiming to challenge sheikhs and billionaires on the football pitch
item.description: Doug King has found a new arena for risk — English football club Coventry City

item.title: Ex-WHSmith high street chain ‘almost completely broken’, says new boss
item.description: TG Jones chief blames woes on former owner’s lack of investment as it seeks to rally support for restructuring plan

item.title: Crypto industry braces for quantum computing threat
item.description: Threat to code that underpins bitcoin has moved ‘from theoretical to credible’, industry figures warn

item.title: Sir Ben Ainslie: ‘Sailing’s going primetime’
item.description: The Olympic champion on SailGP’s rise, America’s Cup reform and turning elite racing into a more investable sport
